title_text: Microsoft Copilot Studio — Transform Operations with Low-Code AI Agents
key_message_text: The era of AI-driven operations is here — build with low-code, deploy on enterprise-grade infrastructure
bullets: Build intelligent AI agents with a low-code interface
Unified with the Microsoft 365 and Power Platform ecosystem
Adoption Guide 2026
notes: Opening slide sets the thesis: Copilot Studio enables any organization to build and deploy AI agents without deep technical expertise, backed by enterprise security.

title_text: Conclusion: Copilot Studio Wins on Speed, Integration, and Security
key_message_text: The only platform that delivers low-code development, enterprise integration, and robust governance simultaneously
bullets: Rapid Build — Deploy AI agents in hours without developers
Seamless Integration — Connect instantly to M365, Dynamics 365, and external APIs
Robust Governance — Maintain enterprise standards with DLP, audit logs, and MIP labels
notes: Pyramid conclusion first: present the three pillars that make Copilot Studio the optimal choice before diving into supporting evidence.

title_text: Reality Check: Three Barriers Block Enterprise AI Adoption
key_message_text: Talent shortages, data silos, and governance gaps are stalling AI initiatives beyond the PoC stage
bullets: Talent Gap — Data scientists and developers are scarce, stalling projects at the PoC stage
Integration Gap — Siloed data and processes prevent organization-wide AI deployment
Governance Gap — Security and compliance standards for generative AI remain undefined
Scale Gap — Transitioning from PoC to production takes too long and costs too much
notes: Frame the problem the audience faces. Each barrier maps directly to a Copilot Studio capability introduced in the next slide.

title_text: Solution: A Platform That Autonomizes Work with Agents and Flows
key_message_text: Conversational AI, task automation, knowledge integration, and multi-channel deployment — all in one platform
bullets: Agents — AI companions that converse, decide, and act autonomously using NLU and generative AI
Agent Flows — Automate repetitive tasks with natural language or a visual editor
Knowledge Integration — Retrieve real-time knowledge from SharePoint, Graph, and external connectors
Multi-Channel Deployment — Publish to Teams, Web, Mobile, WhatsApp, and more
notes: Map each capability to the barriers on the previous slide: agents solve the talent gap, flows solve the scale gap, knowledge solves the integration gap, channels solve reach.

title_text: Choose Your Tool: Agent Builder vs. Copilot Studio by Scope and Need
key_message_text: Use Agent Builder for lightweight Q&A; choose Copilot Studio for advanced workflows and external integrations
bullets: M365 Agent Builder — For individuals and small teams; natural language authoring; SharePoint Q&A focus
Copilot Studio — For departments, organizations, and external customers; multi-step workflows; Premium connectors
Migration Path — Create in Agent Builder → Copy to Copilot Studio to unlock advanced features
Decision Criteria — Select based on target audience size, feature requirements, and governance needs
notes: Based on the official Microsoft decision tree: audience scope, deployment breadth, functionality depth, and governance requirements determine the right tool.

title_text: Proof of Momentum: Six Major Releases in Six Months
key_message_text: GPT-5 support, Computer-Using Agents, MCP integration, and more — rapid innovation validates platform longevity
bullets: Sep 2025 — Computer-Using Agents (CUA) preview, Client SDK for native apps
Oct 2025 — GPT-4.1 as default model, MCP server integration, agent evaluation test sets
Nov 2025 — GPT-5 GA rollout, multi-agent orchestration, SharePoint metadata filters
Jan 2026 — VS Code extension GA, Cloud PC pooling, enhanced evaluation with activity maps
Feb 2026 — Claude Sonnet 4.5 for CUA, prompt builder enhancements with new models
Mar 2026 — Work IQ tool integration (preview) for real-time Microsoft 365 insights
notes: Chronological evidence proving continuous investment. Each release addresses real enterprise needs — governance, developer experience, model choice, and automation scope.

title_text: Adoption Cost: Three Paths to Start with Minimal Risk
key_message_text: Free trial → M365 subscription → Standalone license: a staged approach that minimizes financial risk
bullets: Free Trial — Sign up and start immediately; extend 30 days; agents run 90 days post-expiry
M365 Subscription Included — Teams-only channel, standard connectors, basic capabilities
Standalone Subscription — All channels, Premium connectors, generative orchestration, full ALM
Pay-As-You-Go — Flexible billing via Copilot Credits based on actual consumption
notes: Lower the financial objection by showing a clear on-ramp. The free trial removes all initial cost barriers; the M365 path leverages existing spend.

title_text: Governance: Enterprise-Grade Security Is Built In, Not Bolted On
key_message_text: DLP, audit logs, CMK, and MIP labels are embedded in the platform — no extra configuration for baseline security
bullets: Data Loss Prevention (DLP) — Power Platform data policies control connectors, channels, and generative AI usage
Audit & Monitoring — Microsoft Purview + Sentinel provide full visibility into maker and user activity
Encryption & Data Residency — Customer-managed keys (CMK) and geographic data residency compliance
Sensitivity Labels — MIP labels propagate consistently from SharePoint sources to agent responses
notes: Address the governance barrier head-on. Every control listed is available today, not on a roadmap. Link to the security-and-governance source for credibility.

title_text: Execution Plan: From Trial to Production in 90 Days
key_message_text: A three-phase roadmap that minimizes risk while accelerating time-to-value
bullets: Phase 1 (Days 1–30) — Sign up for free trial; build an IT helpdesk Q&A agent as PoC
Phase 2 (Days 31–60) — Finalize Agent Builder vs. Copilot Studio decision; design DLP policies
Phase 3 (Days 61–90) — Acquire standalone license; deploy to production channels; enable audit logging
notes: Concrete, time-boxed actions remove ambiguity. Each phase maps to a licensing tier introduced on the cost slide.

key_message_text: Sign up, build your first agent, and set up governance — start moving now
bullets: ① Sign up for a free trial at copilotstudio.microsoft.com
② Identify your top use case and build your first agent PoC
③ Partner with IT to design DLP policies and enable audit logging
notes: End with clear, actionable next steps. Each action is achievable within one business day to create immediate momentum.
